message:
chore(beads): mark ft-uwm9x (key_up mux forwarding) as in_progress

Update bead ft-uwm9x status from open to in_progress to reflect that
the T9.2 key_up event forwarding work is now underway.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -4466,7 +4466,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-upg.9.7","title":"Docs: operator playbook (using triage + why + reproduce)","description":"# Task: Document operator playbook

## Goal
Provide a pragmatic operator guide for keeping wa healthy.

## Requirements
- Document common flows:
  - triage → why → fix
  - triage → reproduce → file issue
  - triage → mute/noise control
- Provide copy-paste examples.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new user can recover from common incidents using only the playbook.

-{"id":"ft-uwm9x","title":"[audit][T9.2] Fix key_up handling for mux client panes","description":"# Fix key_up handling for mux client panes

## Problem
frankenterm/client/src/pane/clientpane.rs line 490:
  // TODO: decide how to handle key_up for mux client

Key-up events are not forwarded to remote mux panes. This means key-repeat and modifier-release behaviors may be incorrect for remote panes.

Also line 559:
  // FIXME: retrieve this from the remote

Some pane metadata is stubbed rather than fetched from the remote mux server.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. key_up events forwarded to remote mux panes
2. Remote pane metadata fetched properly
3. Both TODO/FIXME comments removed","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:22.688429Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:54.083549Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","client","mux","vendored"]}

Both TODO/FIXME comments removed","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:22.688429Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:17:29.238192Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","client","mux","vendored"]}
 {"id":"ft-uyve","title":"[EPIC] Saved searches + scheduled alerts","description":"## Background
Operators repeatedly run the same search/event filters to track specific agent states. Today this is manual and easy to forget.

## Goals
- Persist named searches (query + filters + scope) for reuse
- Allow scheduled runs that emit notifications when results appear
- Integrate with CLI/TUI/web dashboards without duplicating search logic

## Non-Goals
- New query language (reuse existing FTS syntax)
- External alert delivery (handled in notification channels epic)

## Considerations
- Saved search must be safe to execute (no side effects)
- Scheduling should be rate-limited and observable
- Outputs must be redacted per safety config

## Success Criteria
- Users can create/list/run/delete saved searches
- Scheduled alerts fire with clear context and can be disabled
- Full unit + e2e test coverage with verbose logs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:03.835297223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.253264Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:29.507396585Z","close_reason":"All child beads completed; closing stale-open epic to keep active plan set non-duplicative.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-uz4s","title":"Fix broken HEAD: add search_explain module declaration","description":"HEAD was broken because main.rs referenced wa_core::search_explain but lib.rs lacked the pub mod declaration. Added module declaration, 3 storage methods needed by search_explain, and fixed clippy or_fun_call warning. Commit: 31a8697","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T17:58:38.984713680Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.250906Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T17:58:48.108662635Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-uzq7","title":"Docs: storage/index tuning + maintenance commands","description":"# Task: Document storage/indexing tuning

## Goal
Make storage behavior predictable and tunable for power users.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - how WAL/checkpointing works in wa
  - how to interpret index lag signals
  - recommended maintenance commands (explicit vacuum/optimize)
  - how to collect perf evidence for bug reports

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can self-diagnose slow search and provide actionable data in an issue.
